Article Title: Lamine Yamal creates history, becomes first teenager to play in both Euro and FIFA World Cup finals
Article Text:
Spain's Lamine Yamal (AP Photo)

Teenager powers Spain's run to the final



Spain stick with winning formula



Spain starting XI: Unai Simon, Pedro Porro, Aymeric Laporte, Pau Cubarsi, Marc Cucurella, Rodri (captain), Fabian Ruiz, Dani Olmo, Lamine Yamal, Mikel Oyarzabal, Bryan Zaragoza.

Lamine Yamal added another remarkable milestone to his rapidly growing legacy even before kick-off in the FIFA World Cup 2026 final, becoming the first player in football history to feature in both a UEFA European Championship final and a FIFA World Cup final as a teenager.The 19-year-old sensation took his place in Spain's starting XI against defending champions Argentina in the title clash at the MetLife Stadium in New York/New Jersey, achieving a feat unmatched by any teenage footballer.Already one of the brightest stars in world football, Yamal had previously featured in Spain's victorious UEFA Euro 2024 final campaign. By stepping onto the field for the World Cup showpiece, he became the first teenager to appear in the finals of both of international football's biggest tournaments.The historic achievement underlines the extraordinary rise of the Barcelona winger, who has become an indispensable figure for Spain despite being just 19 years old.Yamal has been one of the standout performers throughout Spain's World Cup campaign.He played a decisive role in La Roja's 2-0 semifinal victory over France, winning the penalty after dispossessing Lucas Digne that captain Mikel Oyarzabal converted to open the scoring.Pedro Porro added the second after the break following an excellent move involving Dani Olmo, while Yamal also had a goal ruled out for offside.Spain's disciplined defence frustrated France throughout the contest, limiting Kylian Mbappe and Aurelien Tchouameni before sealing a sixth clean sheet in seven matches.The European champions have combined defensive solidity with attacking flair during the tournament and now stand just one victory away from lifting their second FIFA World Cup trophy, having previously triumphed in South Africa in 2010.Spain head coach Luis de la Fuente kept faith with the same starting XI that defeated France in the semifinals.Unai Simon started in goal behind a back four of Pedro Porro, Aymeric Laporte, Pau Cubarsi and Marc Cucurella. Captain Rodri anchored the midfield alongside Fabian Ruiz and Dani Olmo, while Yamal partnered skipper Mikel Oyarzabal and Bryan Zaragoza in attack.Standing between Spain and a second world title were Lionel Messi and defending champions Argentina, who reached the final after a dramatic 2-1 comeback victory over England.Argentina made three changes from that semifinal, recalling Gonzalo Montiel, Rodrigo De Paul and Nicolas Gonzalez to the starting lineup alongside Messi and Julian Alvarez.While much of the spotlight before the final centred on Messi's bid for back-to-back World Cup titles and another piece of history, Yamal quietly etched his own name into the record books.
Article Summary: Lamine yamal becomes the first teenager to feature in both a uefa european championship final and a fifa world cup final as a teenager. The 19-year-old sensation started in spain's starting xi against argentina in the title clash at the metlife stadium in new york/new jersey.

Article Title: Lionel Messi, Lamine Yamal share emotional hug after Spain's FIFA World Cup triumph - WATCH
Article Text:
Lionel Messi and Lamine Yamal (PTI Photo)

Torres scores title-winning goal



'It was a goal scored by 47 million people'



Spain complete dominant performance



NEW DELHI: Lionel Messi and Spain youngster Lamine Yamal shared a heartfelt hug after the final whistle of the FIFA World Cup 2026 final on Sunday, giving fans one of the most memorable moments of the tournament.After Spain beat defending champions Argentina 1-0 in extra time to win the title, Messi and Yamal met on the pitch, embraced each other and had a brief chat. The touching moment quickly went viral on social media.Their hug carried extra meaning because of their famous connection from the 2007 UNICEF photoshoot, when a young Messi was photographed with baby Yamal.At 39, Messi became only the second footballer to play in three FIFA World Cup finals, while 19-year-old Yamal celebrated winning the World Cup in his first final appearance.Spain secured their second FIFA World Cup title thanks to substitute Ferran Torres, who scored the only goal of the match in the 106th minute of extra time.The move began just after the restart of the second period of extra time. Argentina were already down to 10 men after Enzo Fernandez was sent off in the 93rd minute.Lamine Yamal collected the ball on the right wing and passed it to Pedro Porro. Porro then delivered a cross into the penalty area that was almost going out of play.Nico Williams, who had come on in the 75th minute, headed the ball back into the middle, where Torres reacted quickly and fired a left-footed shot past goalkeeper Emiliano Martínez.It was Spain's 20th shot of the match, while Argentina had still not managed a single attempt at goal.Torres said the winning goal belonged to the entire country and reflected on the criticism he had faced during the tournament.“I think at the end of the day, it was a goal scored by 47 million people,” Torres said. “I feel very happy. I was heavily criticized during the World Cup, but I think it was written in destiny and things. But I always have that chance, that option to continue, and God gives it to those who believe it, who deserve it most.”After scoring, the Barcelona forward celebrated by kicking down the corner flag before being surrounded by his teammates as Spain's fans erupted in celebration.Spain controlled the match for long periods and created chance after chance before finally breaking Argentina's resistance in extra time.Marc Cucurella started the move from the back by passing to Eric García, who found Martín Zubimendi. The midfielder then released Yamal on the right wing, leading to the move that eventually produced Torres' winner.Torres almost added a second goal later in the match but was ruled offside. Spain then defended Argentina's late corner kicks to seal a famous victory and lift the World Cup trophy for the second time.
Article Summary: Spain beat argentina 1-0 in extra time to win the fifa world cup 2026. Substitute ferran torres scored the only goal of the match in the 106th minute of extra time. Messi and yamal met on the pitch, embraced each other and had a brief chat. The touching moment quickly went viral on social media.

Article Title: 20 shots to none: How Spain utterly owned Argentina in FIFA World Cup 2026 final and rendered Lionel Messi irrelevant
Article Text: For years, Spanish football has been synonymous with control - of the ball, of space, of tempo. On Sunday night at New York New Jersey Stadium, Spain produced perhaps the ultimate expression of that philosophy, reducing defending champions Argentina and Lionel Messi to mere passengers in the FIFA World Cup 2026 final.Ferran Torres' 106th-minute winner secured a 1-0 victory after extra time and delivered Spain's second FIFA World Cup title. But the scoreline barely hinted at the gulf between the two sides. This was not simply a victory. It was a footballing masterclass that left Argentina chasing shadows for almost two hours.The numbers told the story as emphatically as the result. Spain unleashed 20 shots, 12 of them on target, completed 738 of their 817 passes and forced Emiliano Martínez into one save after another. Argentina, by contrast, failed to muster a single shot in regular time.That extraordinary statistic places the 2026 final in unique company. According to Opta, among nearly 900 World Cup matches dating back to 1966, only three teams have failed to attempt a shot during normal time: Costa Rica against Brazil in 1990, Costa Rica against Spain in 2022, and now Argentina against Spain in the 2026 final. The first two were underdogs expected to survive.The third was the reigning world champion led by Messi.Spain ensured there was never a platform for Argentina to settle. Fabian Ruiz, later Pedri, dictated the rhythm from midfield, Alex Beana and Rodri constantly found pockets between the lines, while Dani Elmo and, later Nico Williams Jr, and Lamine Yamal stretched the pitch with relentless width. Argentina spent most of the night retreating, reorganising and defending.Messi, who had carried Argentina through another deep World Cup run at the age of 39, found himself isolated. Every touch was immediately met by red shirts, every passing lane closed, every attempt to slow the game and impose his rhythm suffocated almost before it began. It was one of the quietest nights of his World Cup career - not because he lacked ambition, but because Spain never allowed the contest to be played on his terms.If anything illustrated Spain's control, it was Argentina's inability to escape their own half. The South Americans finished the 90 minutes with just 34% possession - their lowest share of the ball in any World Cup match since Opta's records began in 1966.Even more revealing, goalkeeper Emiliano Martínez finished as player with most touches for Argentina, underlining how relentlessly Spain pinned the holders back. Spain did not merely outplay Argentina; they monopolised the ball, dictated the territory and deprived the champions of the rhythm that had defined their title defence.Only Martínez prevented the contest from ending much earlier. The Aston Villa goalkeeper produced a string of outstanding saves to deny Williams, Pedri, and Torres, repeatedly frustrating Spain as wave after wave of attacks crashed into Argentina's penalty area.Eventually, however, even Martínez could not withstand the pressure.Sixteen minutes into extra time, Williams managed to cushion a header into the path of Torres, who fired his finish beyond the goalkeeper. It was a goal Spain had threatened all evening and one that finally rewarded their dominance.Argentina's hopes had taken a major dent when Enzo Fernández was sent off in stoppage time, reducing Lionel Scaloni's side to 10 men and extinguishing any realistic prospect of an Argentine surge. Even before the red card, though, the balance of the contest had long been decided.Spain's triumph was built not on moments of brilliance alone but on collective superiority. Luis de la Fuente's side controlled possession, dictated territory and suffocated every avenue Argentina attempted to explore.Their pressing denied transitions, their midfield recycled possession with remarkable composure and their defensive line gave Messi and Julián Álvarez little room to breathe.For Argentina, the defeat marked the end of a reign that began in Qatar four years ago. Whether this ultimately proves to be Messi's final World Cup appearance remains to be seen, but if it is, it closed not with one last moment of genius but with Spain rendering football's greatest player almost anonymous.World Cup finals are often remembered for drama, controversy or moments of individual inspiration. This one will be remembered for something far rarer - complete domination.Spain didn't just beat the defending champions. They made them disappear.
Article Summary: Ferran torres' 106th-minute winner secured a 1-0 victory after extra time. But the scoreline barely hinted at the gulf between the two sides. Spain unleashed 20 shots, 12 of them on target, and completed 738 of their 817 passes. Argentina failed to muster a single shot in regular time.

Article Title: 'Hurts me in the soul': Lionel Scaloni breaks down after Argentina's FIFA World Cup final loss - WATCH
Article Text:
Lionel Scaloni

Uncertain about his future



NEW DELHI: Argentina head coach Lionel Scaloni struggled to hold back tears after his side lost 0-1 to Spain in the FIFA World Cup 2026 final at the New York New Jersey Stadium on Sunday.Scaloni said the defeat was deeply painful as Argentina failed to defend the title they had won in Qatar four years ago. Ferran Torres scored the only goal in extra time to hand Spain their second World Cup crown.Speaking after the match, the Argentina coach said: "We had injuries in key positions that we weren't expecting. The wear and tear has been enormous, they gave their last effort. I'm in a dream place for everyone, I always thank the President (Tapia) for the opportunity. This place is wonderful. It's hard to form a group like this. Losing hurts me in the soul."Scaloni also raised doubts about whether he would continue as Argentina coach. His contract runs until December, and he said he would first speak with Argentina Football Association president Claudio Tapia before making any decision."I will talk to the president," he said. "I have an idea of what I would like to do. I will see out my contract and I feel the need, I don't know if something as big can be done.We need to speak this through."He thanked Tapia for giving him the opportunity to coach the national team and said he needed time to think."I am thankful to the president for bringing me this chance of being in this place where I am at the moment. It was a dream place for everyone. We have tried up until the very last minute to give our utmost, the staff and players. I believe it's only fair that I can take this time for myself to think this through.""Bear with me, I don't know if I can continue. This place is wonderful, it is a dream place."He explained that building another successful team would not be easy."We would never have imagined, including my staff, that we would be in this place. In order to continue you need a great deal of things, especially resetting your mind, rebooting, creating a group like this again. That is very difficult to create again."
Article Summary: Argentina lost 0-1 to spain in the fifa world cup 2026 final on sunday. Ferran torres scored the only goal in extra time to hand spain their second world cup crown. Lionel scaloni said the defeat was deeply painful as argentina failed to defend the title they had won in qatar four years ago.

Article Title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup Final halftime show explained: Why football is getting its own Super Bowl moment for the first time
Article Text:
2026 World Cup Final halftime show explained (Via X)

Why has the FIFA World Cup never had a halftime show before?

Who is performing at the FIFA World Cup 2026 final halftime show?

How long is the FIFA World Cup halftime show and does it break football's rules?

How to watch the 2026 World Cup final halftime show ?

Will the halftime show become a permanent FIFA World Cup tradition?

For 96 years, the FIFA World Cup final had one universal halftime ritual: fifteen minutes, a tunnel, and tactical adjustments. On July 19, 2026, all of that changed. For the first time in the tournament's history, the World Cup final at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey, will feature a full halftime show, headlined by Madonna, Shakira , BTS, and Justin Bieber , curated by Coldplay's Chris Martin. Global Citizen CEO Hugh Evans has called it "the single largest gathering of artists united for a cause since Live Aid." Football's biggest stage just got its biggest interval.The short answer is that football never needed one. Unlike the NFL, which built a decades-long tradition around the Super Bowl halftime show partly to recover from declining ratings in the early 1990s, the FIFA World Cup has always sold itself on the match alone. Billions tune in not for entertainment segments but for the football. That self-sufficiency meant a halftime show was never really on the table.For 96 years, the World Cup final observed the same tradition at halftime: punditry, tactical analysis, and players regrouping in the tunnel. There were closing ceremonies, yes, but those happened after the final whistle. The halftime interval was sacred and brief.FIFA tested the concept at a smaller scale at the 2025 Club World Cup final, with Colombian artist J Balvin, Doja Cat, and Tems headlining, and Coldplay making a surprise appearance.That show also featured Chris Martin as curator and was produced by Global Citizen. It was, in hindsight, a rehearsal.The advent of the superstar-era of Super Bowl halftime shows was conceived in response to a ratings dip in the early 1990s and has since become a staple of the sporting event. FIFA, now broadcasting to the largest television audiences in sports history, finally decided it was their turn.The 2022 final in Qatar was the most-watched in FIFA World Cup history, and the 2026 final in New Jersey is expected to break those records, which explains why adding a halftime show makes commercial sense now when it never did before.The lineup reads like a playlist no algorithm could generate. The show is directed by Hamish Hamilton and co-headlined by Madonna, Shakira, BTS, and Justin Bieber, with Burna Boy, Venezuelan conductor Gustavo Dudamel, the PS22 Chorus, and Coldplay also performing.Madonna brings seven Grammy wins and a career spanning four decades. BTS, returning from mandatory military service after a three-year hiatus, became the first K-pop group to receive a Grammy nomination in 2021. Their World Cup performance will be the largest live audience of their post-enlistment era.Shakira's connection to this tournament runs deeper than any co-headliner. Her 2010 World Cup anthem "Waka Waka (This Time for Africa)" set a Guinness World Record as Spotify's most streamed FIFA World Cup song. She will also perform "Dai Dai," the official song for the 2026 tournament, with Burna Boy, making them the only artists performing at both the opening ceremony and the final.Justin Bieber was a later addition, confirmed on July 8, 2026. He completes what FIFA describes as the biggest halftime lineup it has ever staged.Then there is Gustavo Dudamel. The Venezuelan conductor will lead musicians from the New York Philharmonic and Venezuela's Simón Bolívar Symphony Orchestra. That is not a detail to gloss over. Placing a South American classical conductor front and centre at a World Cup final where Argentina face Spain says something deliberate about the cultural register FIFA wants to strike.FIFA President Gianni Infantino said in a statement: "As the world unites for the most significant football match in history on Sunday, 19 July 2026 at the New York New Jersey Stadium, this groundbreaking spectacle, curated by Chris Martin of Coldplay, will celebrate football, music and our shared values, ensuring a legacy that transcends the final whistle."Uganda's Ghetto Kids dance troupe and Iraqi-born Australian singer Emmanuel Kelly also join the lineup, along with Sesame Street and Muppets characters including Kermit the Frog and Miss Piggy. The children's entertainment inclusion is deliberate: the show is produced by FIFA and Global Citizen, aiming to raise $100 million for the FIFA Global Citizen Education Fund, which works to end extreme poverty and expand access to sports for children.Here is where the story becomes genuinely complicated. The performance itself will last about 11 minutes, but the entire interval is being extended to allow for stage setup, teardown, and additional television broadcast coverage. The full halftime break will stretch to approximately 30 minutes.That is a problem. Under Rule 7.2 of the Laws of the Game, IFAB states: "Players are entitled to an interval at half-time, not exceeding 15 minutes." FIFA has not publicly explained how it plans to comply with that rule, or whether it will simply override it.Sports medicine experts and football officials have warned that prolonged breaks can disrupt players' physical rhythm, and IFAB has previously stated that extending halftime could negatively impact player welfare and safety because of the longer period of inactivity, potentially increasing the risk of injuries when play resumes.The inconsistency is pointed. In 2021, IFAB rejected a request from CONMEBOL to increase halftime to 25 minutes, citing concerns over player welfare. FIFA, the very body that sits within IFAB's governance structure, is now planning an interval twice that length for the biggest match in world football.Critics from inside the game argue this move has drawn comparisons to the Americanization of football, while broadcasters like the BBC and ITV were initially reluctant to air the show, preferring their usual halftime analysis before agreeing to broadcast it.The counterargument, and it is not a weak one, is that this is sport and entertainment finding common ground at exactly the moment the global audience is at its largest. Football may resist commercialisation more fiercely than any other sport, but 96 years without a halftime show is also 96 years of leaving a broadcast opportunity largely unused.The World Cup final kicks off at 3pm ET on Sunday, July 19, at New York New Jersey Stadium. The halftime show is expected to begin at approximately 3:45pm ET, although the exact time will depend on stoppage time in the first half.In the United States, viewers can watch the show live on Fox Sports, with the match airing on the FOX network. Spanish-language viewers can tune in through NBCUniversal's Peacock with a Premium or Premium Plus subscription, with the match also broadcasting on Telemundo.For international audiences, the show will air alongside the World Cup final broadcast in each respective territory. Given that the 2022 final drew close to 1.5 billion viewers, and this year's final is projected to exceed that, there are very few corners of the world where the halftime show will not be visible.Football purists may object. Many will. But tonight, for 11 minutes in New Jersey, the game stops and something entirely new begins. Whether the sport is better for it is a conversation that will run a lot longer than the halftime break itself.That may be the most interesting question of all.The FIFA World Cup final has changed remarkably little over the decades. Rule changes, technology such as VAR and goal-line systems have altered how football is played, but the structure of the match itself has remained largely untouched.The success or failure of the 2026 halftime show could influence how future tournaments are organised.If viewers respond positively and the football experience remains unaffected, FIFA may view the concept as a permanent feature of future finals. It would provide another commercial opportunity while strengthening the tournament's entertainment appeal.If, however, the performance attracts criticism for disrupting players or shifting attention away from the match, pressure could mount to return to the traditional format.Either way, the decision reflects football's changing relationship with global entertainment. The FIFA World Cup no longer exists only as a sporting competition. It is also one of the world's largest cultural events, bringing together billions of viewers from every continent.The challenge for FIFA is finding the balance between innovation and tradition. Football's appeal has always rested on the drama created by the players, not the production surrounding them. The 2026 final will test whether those two worlds can successfully coexist without one overshadowing the other.
Article Summary: Fifa world cup 2026 final will feature a full halftime show for the first time. Madonna, shakira, bts, and justin bieber will perform at metlife stadium in new jersey. Coldplay's chris martin curated the halftime show. The 2026 world cup final will feature an 11-minute halftime show.

Article Title: Awkward scenes! Donald Trump remains on podium during Spain's FIFA World Cup trophy celebrations; Gianni Infantino steps in - WATCH
Article Text:
PTI Photo

Boos greet Trump during trophy ceremony



Torres fires Spain to second World Cup title



Trump's earlier World Cup controversy



NEW DELHI: US President Donald Trump became one of the biggest talking points after Spain defeated Argentina 1-0 to win the FIFA World Cup 2026 final at New York New Jersey Stadium on Sunday.Trump stood alongside the Spain players as they received the World Cup trophy and remained on the podium during the celebrations.Trump only stepped aside after FIFA president Gianni Infantino pulled him away, allowing captain Rodri to lift the trophy with his teammates. The moment quickly went viral on social media.The US President also handed the FIFA World Cup trophy to the Spanish players after the final at New York New Jersey Stadium.Boos echoed around the stadium when Trump and Infantino walked onto the field for the trophy presentation after the match.Trump, who had arrived at the stadium by helicopter, congratulated players with handshakes as they walked across the stage before standing with Spain's squad during the trophy presentation.Spain beat defending champions Argentina 1-0 after extra time thanks to substitute Ferran Torres' goal in the 106th minute.Spain dominated possession and created far more chances throughout the match, while Lionel Messi 's Argentina managed to force extra time before finally conceding.Torres reacted quickest to a loose ball inside the box and fired a left-footed shot under the crossbar to seal Spain's second FIFA World Cup title, their first since 2010.Trump had earlier found himself at the centre of controversy during the tournament after calling FIFA president Gianni Infantino to review the red card shown to US striker Folarin Balogun in the group-stage match against Bosnia and Herzegovina.Balogun's one-match suspension was later lifted, allowing him to play in the Round of 16 against Belgium, where the United States lost 4-1.
Article Summary: Spain defeated argentina 1-0 to win the fifa world cup 2026 final at new york new jersey stadium on sunday. Substitute ferran torres' goal in the 106th minute sealed spain's second world cup title. Us president donald trump stood alongside spain players as they received the world cup trophy and remained on the podium during celebrations.

Article Title: 'He's a disgrace': Argentina star sent off after grabbing Spain player's throat in ugly World Cup final scenes
Article Text:
Spain's Gavi, left, falls as he scuffles with Argentina's Leandro Paredes (5) and Thiago Almada during the World Cup final soccer match between Spain and Argentina in East Rutherford, N.J., near New York, Sunday, July 19, 2026. (AP Photo/Yuki Iwamura)

Tempers had already begun to rise during the match

Referee Slavko Vincic, of Slovenia, shows a red card to Argentina's Enzo Fernandez (24) during the World Cup final soccer match between Spain and Argentina in East Rutherford, N.J., near New York, Sunday, July 19, 2026. (AP Photo/Frank Franklin II)

Spain's celebrations became the flashpoint

Clash with Gavi leads to red card

Players scuffle et the end of the World Cup final soccer match between Spain and Argentina in East Rutherford, N.J., near New York, Sunday, July 19, 2026. (AP Photo/Matt Slocum)

Neville and Shearer condemn Argentina's reaction

Bitter ending to Argentina's title defence

Spain's historic FIFA World Cup triumph ended amid chaotic scenes after the final whistle, with Argentina midfielder Leandro Paredes shown a straight red card following an ugly confrontation involving several players from both teams.Luis de la Fuente's side defeated defending champions Argentina 1-0 after extra time at the New York New Jersey Stadium to lift the 2026 FIFA World Cup, with Ferran Torres scoring the decisive goal. But moments after Spain's celebrations began, tensions boiled over into a mass confrontation that resulted in Paredes being dismissed and left an otherwise memorable final overshadowed by controversy.Although the final produced just one goal across 120 minutes, the contest was fiercely contested throughout.Argentina had already been reduced to 10 men late in normal time when Enzo Fernández received his second yellow card after a reckless challenge on Spain defender Pau Cubarsí.Earlier in extra time, Lionel Messi also became involved in an exchange with referee Slavko Vinčić, arguing that Marc Cucurella should be punished after appearing to speak while covering his mouth during an on-field exchange.Those incidents set the tone for an increasingly heated conclusion before emotions spilled over completely once the final whistle confirmed Spain as world champions.As soon as Vinčić ended the match, Spain's substitutes sprinted from the bench onto the pitch to celebrate with their teammates.According to reports, Argentina defender Nahuel Molina appeared to swing an arm towards Spain captain Rodri as the midfielder ran past him during the celebrations.Rodri immediately stopped and confronted Molina, with both players exchanging words before Spain defender Eric García stepped between them in an attempt to calm the situation.Instead, the confrontation escalated.Paredes rushed towards the scene and allegedly grabbed García by the throat before shoving him as players from both teams converged around the incident.Argentina manager Lionel Scaloni quickly arrived and was seen attempting to separate players while gesturing for calm, but the confrontation continued to spread across the pitch.With one confrontation still unfolding, another quickly developed nearby.Spain midfielder Gavi had become entangled with Argentina's Thiago Almada when Paredes turned his attention towards the young Spaniard.Television footage appeared to show Paredes wrestling Gavi to the ground before grabbing him by his substitute's bib and pushing into his face.Several Spain players immediately rushed across to shield Gavi, while striker Borja Iglesias was among those who confronted the Argentina midfielder.Witnessing the sequence unfold, referee Vinčić had little hesitation.Paredes, who had already been booked during the match and had earlier escaped what many believed could have been a second caution, was shown a straight red card after the final whistle for his involvement in the altercation.The scenes drew immediate criticism from television pundits covering the final.Speaking on ITV, former England captain Gary Neville said: "He's a disgrace."I mean he was lucky not to be sent off during the game. I love the competitiveness of the Argentinians and they deserve great credit for what they've done in this tournament. Fighting and scrapping for their lives every game."Even today when they were outplayed by a great football side. Messi's propped them up at times and they're very competitive and I love that element of them."But he's just been a disgrace the last couple of games and why the referees have allowed him to continue to do what he's doing."BBC pundit Alan Shearer was equally critical after reviewing the footage."Paredes threw two or three haymakers into someone's face. He went for them," Shearer said."There is no place or space for that."We know how much it means to them and we know how much it hurts to lose, but there is a way to lose. Too many times we have seen that from Argentina. The reaction after the final whistle is terrible."The chaotic finale brought a disappointing conclusion to Argentina's attempt to retain the World Cup.Lionel Scaloni's side had fought through a demanding knockout campaign and reached a second consecutive World Cup final, but they were second best against a Spain side that dominated possession and eventually found the breakthrough in extra time through Ferran Torres.As Spain celebrated becoming world champions for a second time, Argentina's players were left to reflect not only on the defeat but also on an ill-tempered ending that culminated in Paredes' dismissal after the final whistle.For Lionel Messi, who was widely expected to be making his final World Cup appearance, the evening ended with visible emotion as he applauded Argentina's travelling supporters before collecting his runners-up medal, while the post-match disorder involving several teammates threatened to overshadow what had been another remarkable tournament for the defending champions.
Article Summary: Spain defeated argentina 1-0 after extra time to win the 2026 fifa world cup. Ferran torres scored the decisive goal at the new york new jersey stadium. After the match, tensions boiled over into a mass confrontation that resulted in leandro paredes being dismissed for his involvement in the altercation.

Article Title: Lionel Messi exits FIFA World Cup in tears and as football's greatest
Article Text:
Argentina's Lionel Messi (10) during the World Cup final match between Spain and Argentina in East Rutherford, N.J., near New York, Sunday, July 19, 2026. (AP Photo)

Tears rolled down Lionel Messi 's face as he walked away with a silver medal around his neck. Spain had beaten Argentina 1-0 after extra time in the FIFA World Cup final, ending the defending champions' title defence and what could well be the last World Cup appearance of the greatest footballer the game has seen.Twenty years after making his World Cup debut, the 39-year-old could not produce one more miracle. Argentina, chasing back-to-back World Cup titles, were outplayed by Spain for long periods at MetLife Stadium, where 80,663 spectators had largely turned up hoping to watch their Messi-ah lift the trophy once again.Instead, it was Spain celebrating their second World Cup title.Ferran Torres scored just 37 seconds into the second half of extra time to settle the final after Spain had dominated the contest from the opening whistle. As Spanish players celebrated, Messi stood with his teammates, struggling to hold back tears while Argentina's supporters applauded them from the stands.Messi, head bowed, hands on his hips, watched 19-year-old Lamine Yamal embrace Torres after the winning goal. Moments later, with tears running down his cheeks, he collected his runners-up medal while Spain celebrated amid confetti and fireworks."I hope that everyone feels proud of him, of what he’s achieved because he’s the best football player ever stepping foot on a pitch," Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni said after the match.The statistics reflected Spain's dominance in the final. Luis de la Fuente's side outshot Argentina 20-2 and completed 845 passes to Argentina's 433. According to Opta, the 18-shot difference was the second biggest in a World Cup final since records began in 1966, behind only West Germany's 23-1 shot advantage over Argentina in the 1990 final.Spain also succeeded where few teams have ever managed to -- they kept Messi quiet."The first idea was to keep Messi at bay," Spain coach Luis de la Fuente said.Messi managed only 15 touches in the first half and finished with 54 touches in the match. He never found the space to create the moments that had carried Argentina through the tournament. His passes into dangerous areas failed to connect and he rarely received the ball in positions from where he could threaten Spain's defence.That had not been the story of Argentina's campaign until the final.After leaving European football for Major League Soccer three years ago, some questioned whether Messi's best days were behind him. Instead, he led Argentina back to another World Cup final with eight goals and four assists.His eight goals helped Argentina through the group stage and knockout rounds. Against England in the semifinal, he set up both goals in a stoppage-time victory. Against Egypt, with Argentina trailing 2-0 and facing elimination, he provided the cross for the first goal before scoring the equaliser himself.Throughout the tournament, Messi once again showed his ability to influence matches despite covering less ground than his teammates. He walked for nearly half the time he spent on the pitch, averaging around five kilometres per game, conserving energy before producing bursts of quality whenever Argentina needed him.Against Spain, though, that moment never arrived.If this was his last, then Messi ends his World Cup career with 21 goals, a record that stood until France's Kylian Mbappe scored twice in the third-place playoff to move to 22. He also became the first player to start three World Cup finals and has 125 international goals, second only to Cristiano Ronaldo's 146.His achievements extend far beyond statistics.World Cup winner in 2022. Consecutive Copa América titles. Four UEFA Champions League trophies. Twelve league titles across spells with Barcelona and Paris Saint-Germain, only player in football history to win 8 Ballon d'Or awards. A career that placed him alongside Diego Maradona and Pelé in football's greatest-ever debates.As the final whistle blew, Spanish players queued up to console him. Several hugged him before Argentina collected their silver medals. When Spain formed a guard of honour for the runners-up, Messi tried to smile but could not. His expression barely changed as opponents patted him on the back while he walked to the podium.If this was indeed his final appearance on football's biggest stage, it ended without the fairytale finish many had hoped for.It was not because Messi failed to try. Spain simply gave him no room to operate. Their Rodri-led midfield controlled possession, limited Argentina's attacks and prevented Messi from dictating the game the way he had done throughout the tournament.The final may not have delivered the ending he wanted, but it does little to change how his career will be remembered.It may not have been the ending Messi imagined. It may not even be the end. Whether this proves to be Messi's final World Cup appearance or not will become clear only when he decides. But if this was the last image of him on football's biggest stage, it was one of tears, not triumph.However, the defeat in New Jersey cannot undo a career that has already secured its place among the greatest the sport has known. If this is to be his tearful sign off, fans will still look back at having been blessed by football's greatest of all time.
Article Summary: Spain beat argentina 1-0 after extra time in the fifa world cup final. Ferran torres scored just 37 seconds into the second half of extra time to settle the final. Argentina, chasing back-to-back world cup titles, were outplayed by spain for long periods.

Article Title: Will Lionel Messi retire? Argentina coach breaks silence after FIFA World Cup 2026 final defeat
Article Text:
Lionel Messi (PTI Photo)

Spain end Argentina's reign



Torres and Yamal shine for Spain



Spain's complete team effort



NEW DELHI: Will Lionel Messi retire? Was the FIFA World Cup 2026 final his last appearance on football's biggest stage?Those questions dominated discussions as Argentina took on Spain in the final at the New York New Jersey Stadium. Argentina's title defence ended with a 1-0 extra-time defeat, but head coach Lionel Scaloni made it clear that Messi's future would be entirely his own decision.After the final, Scaloni praised the 39-year-old captain and said Messi would continue playing for as long as he wanted."Well, he's 39 years old now. Unbelievable. There's nothing new to discover from my side. It was crystal clear for me that he's going to play up until he decided to do so, and that all the team players, team-mates, and all of his people were going to support him. And I'm sure I hope that everyone feels proud of him, of what he's achieved, because he's the best football player ever," Scaloni said as quoted by The Athletic.Scaloni's comments came after Argentina fell short in their bid to defend the World Cup title they won in Qatar in 2022.Spain defeated Argentina 1-0 after extra time to lift their second FIFA World Cup title and their first since 2010.Substitute Ferran Torres scored the only goal of the match in the 106th minute, beating Emiliano Martinez with a powerful finish after a move involving Lamine Yamal, Pedro Porro and Nico Williams.The victory completed a remarkable campaign for Spain, who went unbeaten throughout the tournament and conceded only one goal in eight matches.Torres also made history by becoming only the fifth substitute to score in a FIFA World Cup final and the first since Germany's Mario Götze scored the winner against Argentina in the 2014 final.He also became just the second Barcelona player to score in a World Cup final after Andres Iniesta's extra-time winner for Spain in 2010.Nineteen-year-old Lamine Yamal lifted the World Cup in the biggest moment of his young career, just two years after helping Spain win UEFA Euro 2024.Spain's title-winning campaign was built on strong performances across the squad.Rodri controlled the midfield throughout the tournament, Mikel Oyarzabal finished with five goals to end among the leading scorers, while goalkeeper Unai Simon won the Golden Glove after a string of impressive displays.Spain started their campaign with a goalless draw against Cape Verde before beating Saudi Arabia 4-0 and Uruguay 1-0 to top Group H.They then defeated Austria 3-0 in the Round of 32, edged Portugal 1-0 in the Round of 16, beat Belgium 2-1 in the quarter-finals and overcame France 2-0 in the semi-finals.In the final, Spain dominated possession, created far more chances than Argentina and defeated the defending champions to seal another World Cup crown.
Article Summary: Argentina lost 1-0 to spain in the fifa world cup 2026 final in new york. Head coach lionel scaloni says lionel messi will continue playing as long as he wants. Ferran torres scored the only goal of the match in the 106th minute, beating emiliano martinez.

Article Title: Ferran Torres only the second player to achieve this rare FIFA World Cup final feat
Article Text:
Ferran Torres (PTI Photo)

Spain dominate but struggle to find breakthrough



Torres delivers the title-winning goal



NEW DELHI: Spain substitute Ferran Torres etched his name into FIFA World Cup history after scoring the winning goal in the 106th minute of the FIFA World Cup 2026 final against Argentina at the New York New Jersey Stadium on Sunday.With the strike, Torres became only the second substitute to score the winning goal in a World Cup final, matching Germany's Mario Götze, who also achieved the feat against Argentina in the 2014 final.Torres came off the bench in the 62nd minute and made the decisive impact as Spain defeated defending champions Argentina 1-0 after extra time to lift their second FIFA World Cup title. Spain's first World Cup triumph came in 2010.Spain controlled the final for long periods, enjoying most of the possession and creating several chances. They continued to push forward while Argentina, led by Lionel Messi , defended deep and managed to take the match into extra time despite being under constant pressure.By the time extra time began, Spain had already created far more chances but had been unable to beat Argentina goalkeeper Emiliano Martínez.The breakthrough finally arrived in the 106th minute. Nico Williams kept a long cross alive by heading the ball into the middle of the penalty area, where Torres reacted quickest.The Spanish forward struck a left-footed shot that flew under the crossbar and into the net, giving Martínez no chance.It was Spain's 20th shot of the match and Torres' first goal of the tournament. His goal proved enough to secure a famous 1-0 victory and Spain's second World Cup crown.Spain held on comfortably to end Argentina's reign as world champions and lift football's biggest trophy for the second time.
Article Summary: Ferran torres scores winning goal in the 106th minute of the fifa world cup 2026 final against argentina. Spain defeated argentina 1-0 after extra time to lift their second world cup title. Torres became only the second substitute to score the winning goals in a world cup final.

What is at stake in the FIFA World Cup 2026 final?

World Cup 2026: Argentina vs Spain date, time, venue FIFA World Cup 2026 final between defending champions Argentina and European champions Spain will be played on July 19 at New York New Jersey Stadium. Lionel Messi is chasing another piece of history and Spain is looking to win its second World Cup so fans everywhere are ready for a final that should have great soccer, contrasting styles and a global audience.The World Cup final will kick off at, atHere are the key start times in major regions:The match will be officiated by, who takes charge of the tournament's biggest fixture.Argentina's road to the championship match has been anything but comfortable. How many FIFA World Cup Golden Boots does Lionel Messi have?

Lionel Messi’s World Cup goals and closest Golden Boot finish

Lionel Messi has won almost every major trophy in football, but there is one FIFA World Cup award that has always stayed out of his reach. Despite scoring 13 goals across five World Cups and leading Argentina to the 2022 title, Messi has never won the FIFA World Cup Golden Boot. His closest chance came in Qatar, where he scored seven goals but finished one behind Kylian Mbappé, who ended the tournament with eight.The FIFA World Cup Golden Boot is given to the player who scores the most goals during the tournament. The award has gone to some of football’s biggest names over the years, but Lionel Messi has never been on the winners list.*Shared award.**Six players finished as joint top scorers.Lionel Messi has never won the FIFA World Cup Golden Boot. His best chance came at the 2022 World Cup in Qatar. Messi scored seven goals and also provided three assists as Argentina lifted the trophy. However, Kylian Mbappé scored a hat-trick in the final against Argentina to finish the tournament with eight goals and claim the Golden Boot.Although Messi missed out on the top scorer award, he was named the FIFA World Cup Golden Ball winner as the tournament’s best player.It was the second time he received that honor after first winning it at the 2014 World Cup in Brazil.Messi finished his World Cup career with 13 goals, making him Argentina’s all-time top scorer in the tournament. He scored once in 2006, none in 2010, four in 2014, one in 2018 and seven in 2022.Even with that impressive record, he never finished as the tournament’s highest scorer. His seven goals in Qatar remain his closest attempt at winning the Golden Boot. Instead, that World Cup will always be remembered for helping him complete his career by lifting the one trophy he wanted most.
Article Summary: Lionel messi has never won the fifa world cup golden boot. His best chance came at the 2022 world cup in qatar, where he scored seven goals but finished one behind kylian mbappé, who ended the tournament with eight. The award is given to the player who scores the most goals during the tournament.

Article Title: IShowSpeed’s wild reaction to BTS steals attention during FIFA World Cup Final halftime show
Article Text:
IShowSpeed’s wild reaction to BTS steals attention during FIFA World Cup Final halftime show (Image via Getty)

IShowSpeed goes all out as BTS perform ‘Dynamite’ at FIFA World Cup Final

Why IShowSpeed’s BTS reaction meant so much to ARMYs?

The first-ever FIFA World Cup Final halftime show created plenty of memorable moments, but one unexpected reaction grabbed almost as much attention as the performance itself. Popular streamer IShowSpeed, whose real name is Darren Watkins Jr. , was caught on camera celebrating wildly as BTS appeared on stage during the Spain vs. Argentina final at MetLife Stadium on July 19, 2026. A fan account on X, @solmerv, shared the clip with the caption, “SPEED’S REACTION TO BTS’ PERFORMANCE HELP 😭 #BTSxWorldCup2026.” The short video spread across social media, with many fans enjoying the crossover between one of the world’s biggest streamers and one of the world’s biggest music groups.The video, which is about 15 seconds long, shows Speed sitting in the stadium stands before suddenly jumping to his feet as BTS appear on the giant screens. He points excitedly toward the stage, dances with full energy, throws his arms around, shouts with his mouth wide open and looks completely caught up in the moment.People sitting around him can also be seen watching the performance. Some smile at his reaction, while others appear to record the moment on their phones.The packed stadium, giant screens and halftime visuals can all be seen in the background.BTS performed “Dynamite” wearing matching black-and-red outfits during the star-filled halftime show. The performance also featured artists including Madonna, Justin Bieber, Shakira and Burna Boy, making it one of the biggest entertainment events of the tournament.The reaction did not surprise many fans who have followed Speed recently. During one of his live streams before the World Cup Final, he openly said that BTS is his favourite K-pop group, while also mentioning that his brother prefers Stray Kids. Because of that, many BTS fans were happy to see his genuine excitement when the group took the stage.Speed is one of the world’s most popular online creators, known for his loud and energetic football streams, gaming videos and entertaining reactions. He has spent much of the 2026 FIFA World Cup attending matches in person and sharing his experiences with millions of viewers. He also released his own World Cup-themed song, “Champions,” during the tournament.The halftime show itself also carried special meaning. It was the first halftime show in FIFA World Cup Final history, bringing a Super Bowl-style music performance to football’s biggest match. For BTS, it was another major global appearance following the group’s reunion after military service and during their Arirang world tour.Speed’s reaction added an unexpected highlight to an already historic night, giving fans of football, streaming and K-pop a fun moment that quickly became one of the most talked-about clips from the final.
Article Summary: Ishowspeed’s wild reaction to bts steals attention during fifa world cup final halftime show. Popular streamer ishowspeed was caught on camera celebrating wildly as bts appeared on stage during the spain vs argentina final at metlife stadium on july 19, 2026.

Article Title: Lamine Yamal’s girlfriend Inés García Santos sends heartwarming message as Spain claim World Cup victory over Argentina
Article Text:
Lamine Yamal and gir​lfriend Inés García Santos. Image via: fcbarcelona​

Lamine Yamal's girlfriend Inés García Santos celebrates Spain's World Cup triumph with touching post

Fans react to Inés García Santos' sweet message to Lamine Yamal

Spain defeated Argentina to secure their second World Cup victory

Lamine Yamal's girlfriend, Inés García Santos, posted an emotional Instagram message after Spain beat Argentina 1-0 in the World Cup final on Sunday at MetLife Stadium in New Jersey.The 19-year-old winger helped lift Spain's second World Cup title, and García Santos celebrated the achievement publicly just hours after the final whistle. The moment added to a night already defined by Yamal's rise as one of the sport's brightest young stars.Inés García Santos shared the message on her Instagram account shortly after Spain's win, writing "Lo conseguiste. Enhorabuena mi amor, eres campeón del mundo 🤎," which translates to "You did it. Congratulations my love, you are the champion of the world." She also shared a photo of the couple showing their celebration.Yamal responded publicly with a short reply of his own, writing "te amo mi amor🤎," meaning "I love you my love."The two reportedly met on social media, and García Santos, a fashion and lifestyle influencer, has previously pushed back on assumptions about how the relationship started, insisting there was no cinematic origin story behind it.The couple went public in 2026, though the exact timeline of their relationship remains unclear.After the final whistle, Yamal was also seen celebrating on the pitch with his brother, Keyne, capping off a night that mixed personal milestones with a historic team achievement.The post drew a flood of comments from fans within the hour, many framing García Santos as a good-luck charm for Spain's run:lamineismo_: "lo dije antes del mundial, el amuleto Inés😍 gracias por acompañar a lamine en este fantástico camino Inés❤️" ("I said it before the World Cup, the amulet Inés, thank you for accompanying Lamine on this fantastic journey")mrmar0: "This Girl brought Luck to the Our Golden Boy ✨🏆"sxgarra10: "LA PAREJA DEL AÑO!!!!! ❤️❤️❤️" ("The couple of the year")berneese_: "You've touched more World Cup than Ronaldo, Go girl👏😍"khalid.trkmn: "Lamine yamal should marry her she brought a good luck for him"palmtreepancakes_14: "You guys look so cute together. Hope you guys get married when you're older."The romance played out against the backdrop of a tightly contested final. Luis de la Fuente's Spain dominated for long stretches, limiting Argentina to just two shots across 120 minutes before Ferran Torres broke the deadlock with a 106th-minute strike in extra time. Argentina played the closing stages a man down after midfielder Enzo Fernandez picked up a second yellow card in the 93rd minute.The win gives Spain their second World Cup title, coming 16 years after their first triumph in South Africa in 2010, when Andres Iniesta's extra-time winner sealed the trophy. De la Fuente praised Yamal's contribution afterward, saying the teenager "played great football that will help him to mature and be even a greater footballer than he already is."At 19 years and six days old, Yamal became one of the youngest players in history to win both the World Cup and the European Championship, having already lifted the Euros in 2024. He has now started 13 major tournament matches for Spain and won every one of them.
Article Summary: Spain defeated argentina 1-0 in the world cup final on sunday. Ferran torres scored the only goal of the game in extra time. Lamine yamal's girlfriend, inés garcía santos, posted a touching message to him. The 19-year-old winger helped lift spain's second world cup title.

Article Title: All the A-list celebrities spotted at the 2026 FIFA World Cup Final: From Tom Cruise to Kylie Jenner
Article Text:
All the A-list celebrities spotted at the 2026 FIFA World Cup Final: From Tom Cruise to Kylie Jenner (Getty Images)

Beyoncé and Jay-Z

Timothée Chalamet and Kylie Jenner

Rihanna

Tom Cruise

David Beckham

President Donald Trump

Shakira

Madonna

MrBeast

Who else was spotted at the 2026 FIFA World Cup Final?

MetLife Stadium hosted more than a football match on Sunday. With Argentina taking on Spain in the 2026 FIFA World Cup Final in East Rutherford, New Jersey, the venue became one of the most star-studded arenas on the planet, drawing Hollywood actors, music royalty, sitting heads of state and sporting legends into the same building. The closing ceremony featured performances from Jennifer Hudson, Post Malone and Robbie Williams, while the halftime show – a first in World Cup history – was curated by Coldplay's Chris Martin and featured Madonna, Shakira, Justin Bieber and BTS. Here's a look at every major name spotted on the day.Jay-Z had just wrapped his massive "Extra Innings" concert series at Yankee Stadium the previous week, with Beyoncé making a guest appearance, and the couple carried that momentum straight to MetLife Stadium. The two were spotted cheering in the stands throughout the match, one of the most recognizable couples in music watching one of the most consequential matches in football history.The couple was photographed together at MetLife Stadium ahead of kickoff, continuing what has become a familiar pattern of high-profile sporting appearances together. From NBA playoff games to Wimbledon, the two have consistently shown up at major events this year.The World Cup Final was simply the biggest stage yet.Rihanna attended the final alongside ASAP Rocky, adding to a list of musicians in the stands that already read like a festival lineup. Her appearance at MetLife came with no performance attached -- just one of the biggest names in pop watching football from the crowd like everyone else.Tom Cruise did far more than attend. The actor appeared on the pitch during the closing ceremony and delivered a speech reflecting on the 48 teams that had competed across Canada, Mexico and the United States before introducing the two finalists. He was later photographed in the stands with David, Victoria and Cruz Beckham -- a remarkably cinematic image even by World Cup standards.David Beckham attended the final alongside wife Victoria and brought sons Romeo and Cruz, as well as Romeo's girlfriend Kim Turbull and Cruz's girlfriend Jackie Apostel — practically a family outing at the world's biggest sporting event. Beckham, who has long maintained close ties with FIFA, was one of the more expected names on the guest list.President Trump attended the final alongside First Lady Melania Trump, Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney and Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum. The diplomatic optics were hard to miss --- the three host nations represented in one suite, watching the match that their combined countries had spent years building toward.Shakira was part of the first-ever halftime show at a men's World Cup final, performing alongside Madonna, Justin Bieber and BTS in support of the FIFA Global Citizen Education Fund. For Shakira specifically, this was personal. The Colombia-born global superstar has always had a complicated, emotional connection to football and to Argentina, and performing at a final between Spain and Argentina carried weight that went beyond just the stage.Madonna appeared on stage during the halftime show alongside Brazilian football legends Ronaldinho and Ronaldo, turning the interval into something far beyond a musical interlude. It was the kind of crossover between sport and entertainment that FIFA had been signalling for years, and the final delivered on it fully.Jimmy Donaldson, known as MrBeast, attended the final with his partner Thea Booysen, continuing his recent pattern of appearing at major global events. His presence in the crowd reflected a broader shift in who gets invited to sport's biggest stages — creators and digital celebrities now sitting alongside Hollywood A-listers and world leaders.The guest list ran deep. Matt Damon and wife Luciana, Will Ferrell, Kevin Hart, Austin Butler, Serena Williams, Pharrell Williams, Javier Bardem, Jon Hamm, Scott Eastwood, Adrien Brody and Trevor Noah were all photographed at MetLife Stadium. NBA stars Victor Wembanyama, Karl-Anthony Towns, Jalen Brunson, James Harden, Draymond Green and Tyrese Haliburton also made the trip to East Rutherford. King Felipe VI and Queen Letizia of Spain and Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez were among the political dignitaries present, alongside Spanish football legends Sergio Ramos, Marcos Llorente and Jesus Navas.
Article Summary: The 2026 fifa world cup final was held at metlife stadium in new jersey on sunday. Tom cruise appeared on the pitch during the closing ceremony and introduced the two finalists. The halftime show was curated by coldplay's chris martin and featured madonna, shakira and bts.

Article Title: Logan Paul and Tom Brady's rivalry reaches new low after viral World Cup final confrontation
Article Text:
Logan Paul a​nd Tom Brady. Image via: AP Photos​

Tom Brady and Logan Paul reignite feud with shocking middle-finger exchange at FIFA World Cup final

What's going on between Tom Brady and Logan Paul

What are fans saying about the Tom Brady and Logan Paul feud?

Tom Brady and Logan Paul 's ongoing feud escalated again Sunday when the two exchanged middle fingers in the stands at the 2026 World Cup final between Spain and Argentina at MetLife Stadium.Fanatics CEO Michael Rubin, who filmed the incident for his Instagram, stepped in to separate the pair before the confrontation went further. The moment came less than 48 hours after Brady slapped Paul during a face-to-face staredown at Fanatics Fest in New York, adding fresh fuel to a rivalry that has simmered for months.Video circulating on X showed Brady flashing the gesture toward Paul, who returned it immediately, while Rubin worked to keep the two apart. Brady reportedly stayed composed and smiling throughout the exchange, while Paul pushed to close the distance between them. The scene played out just two days after Karl-Anthony Towns had to step in during their Fanatics Fest confrontation to keep the situation from boiling over.Check out the video here:The tension traces back to the Fanatics Flag Football Classic, where Brady tossed a football at Paul after the whistle, sparking a heated exchange on the field. Paul later needled Brady online over the flag football matchup, prompting Friday's slap , which Paul addressed publicly by calling it a bad example to set and noting he'd suffered a torn tricep. Brady fired back on social media, telling Paul he'd "try again next time" and adding a jab of his own.The friction isn't new. Brady previously downplayed Paul's standing as an athlete during an appearance on the "Impaulsive" podcast, comparing him unfavorably to NFL stars like Saquon Barkley and Justin Jefferson. Paul has since become a fixture on WWE's Raw brand, and Brady has made clear he wants in on that world too, saying this week he's waiting on WWE president Nick Khan to build him a storyline so he can step into the ring for at least one match.Reaction online split between amusement and skepticism, with fans debating whether the whole saga is building toward something bigger:Captain Caleb (@captaincaleb18): "Alright they are dragging this out now"Pablo (@garzavilo): "Who tf does Logan think he is to insult the goat ?"Nate Svoboda (@gr8tsworld): "Brady has seven rings. Logan has seven camera angles. 😂"〽️Bonz〽️ (@DonBonza): "As if there weren't enough reasons to love Tom Brady"TB EGO (@TomBradyEgo, parody account): "Like it or not, Tom Brady knows how to stay relevant"Matthew (@time34c): "Clearly fake but Tom just holding the finger is gold"Whether scripted or not, the rivalry now has real momentum heading toward a possible WWE payoff. Brady has said publicly he's angling for a match, and Paul's growing role on Raw makes the promotion an obvious stage for a resolution.Nothing is confirmed yet, but with two viral incidents in one week, Nick Khan may not have much of a choice left.
Article Summary: Tom brady and logan paul exchanged middle fingers at the 2026 world cup final. Fanatics ceo michael rubin stepped in to separate the pair before the confrontation went further. The moment came less than 48 hours after brady slapped paul at fanatics fest.

Article Title: The Caitlin Clark problem: Indiana Fever guard facing $1,000 penalty sparks concern amid stunning WNBA run
Article Text:
Catilin Clark yelling at referee's face. Image via: Getty Images

Caitlin Clark's technical foul issue has become impossible to ignore

What Stephanie White said about Caitlin Clark's technical foul issue

How more technical fouls could impact Caitlin Clark

Don’t get it wrong: Caitlin Clark is one of the biggest WNBA stars right now, if not the biggest, but there have been some concerning issues she must avoid to maintain her image. So, here’s what’s happening: Clark picked up her sixth technical foul of the season during Indiana's 108-88 win over the New York Liberty on Saturday, triggering a $1,000 fine and pushing her closer to the WNBA's automatic suspension threshold. The foul came after Clark objected to a no-call on a screen involving Jonquel Jones.The technical is Clark's second-most in the league, tied with Phoenix's Kahleah Copper and trailing only Atlanta's Angel Reese, who has seven. Under league rules, players are suspended one game upon reaching eight technicals, with an additional suspension for every two after that.The frustration boiled over early in Saturday's game when Clark and Jones made hard contact on a screen. Clark appealed to the officials, the officials disagreed, and Indiana's star guard was hit with the technical instead. It's part of a pattern that has intensified in recent weeks, including a separate incident against the Golden State Valkyries that drew criticism for how Clark addressed officiating and could bring its own fine. Yahoo Sports summed up the moment plainly, posting that Caitlin Clark is facing a $1,000 fine from the WNBA after the sixth technical.The technicals have piled up against a backdrop of otherwise elite production. Clark dropped 45 points and 10 assists one night before the Liberty game, setting multiple league and franchise records in the process.Fever coach Stephanie White confirmed the technical fouls are now a topic of internal discussion. Asked about Clark's approach and the looming suspension threshold, White said the team is making sure that we're strategic about how Clark handles her frustration on the floor."I didn't ask for an explanation. Certainly there are conversations … there have to be conversations … It's a it's a long season … certainly after you get to the eight … So (we're) making sure that we're strategic … using our technical fouls," White said in a post-game press conference.White also gave an encouraging update on Clark physically, saying she came through the back-to-back weekend without concern, even while looking occasionally off balance late in the second game."She seemed to be feeling pretty good. There were times where I felt like she looked like she was a little off balance, but nothing that I thought was concerning," White said. “I thought she did a really good job of taking what they gave he. … She wasn't making the same kind of shot she was making yesterday, but I think that's to be expected on a back-to-back for the first time in a couple seasons," the Fever coach added.Indiana sits fifth in the league at 16-10, holding a half-game lead over Atlanta for the top spot in the Eastern Conference. Two more technicals would trigger Clark's first suspension, a real risk given the frequency of the fouls and the season's second half still to play.Lakers legend Kareem Abdul-Jabbar pushed back recently on the framing of Clark as the league's singular face, arguing Clark is a very good, possibly even a great, player while noting the label overlooks players like three-time champion A'ja Wilson. Between the fines, the officiating friction and the outside commentary, Clark now carries a career total of $5,200 in WNBA penalties, $5,000 of it tied to technical fouls. That’s a big number in the league in terms of tech foul penalty.Indiana returns to action July 22 against the Connecticut Sun, its final test before the All-Star break, with Clark's technical count now a genuine subplot to the Fever's playoff push.
Article Summary: Caitlin clark picked up her sixth technical foul of the season during indiana's 108-88 win over the new york liberty on saturday. The foul came after clark objected to a no-call on a screen involving jonquel jones. The technical is clark's second-most in the league, tied with phoenix's kahleah copper and trailing only atlanta's angel reese, who has seven.

Article Title: “What a legend”: Internet can't get enough of what JJ Watt did for a fan who respected his privacy
Article Text:
Internet can't get enough of what JJ Watt did for a fan who respected his privacy.(Getty)

JJ Watt turns a missed opportunity into a lifelong memory for one supporter

JJ Watt gave NFL fans another reason to admire him with his kind gesture. A simple breakfast outing in New York City turned into one of the day's most talked-about stories after a supporter shared an honest post on X. Instead of walking up to the former Houston Texans star, he chose to respect Watt's privacy.That decision could have ended the story. Instead, JJ Watt noticed the post himself and made sure the fan did not leave without a memory that would last a lifetime. The exchange quickly spread across social media, with fans praising the former NFL star for taking time out of his day.The story began when X user Douglas Sandstorm revealed he had breakfast next to JJ Watt in Greenwich Village, New York City, on the morning of July 18. Although he had admired Watt for years, he decided not to interrupt him while he ate alone.In his post on X, Sandstorm wrote, "Not proud of it, man enough to say it. I had breakfast this morning in Greenwich Village NYC and a man sat next to me on his own. Somebody I idolised growing up watching NFL. But out of respect... I didn't even ask for a picture."The message soon reached Watt. Less than an hour later, the three-time NFL Defensive Player of the Year replied on X and asked whether the fan was still nearby.Sandstorm immediately changed his plans and met Watt for the photo he never thought he would get.After the meeting, Sandstorm shared another post on X alongside their picture. "What a legend bro! Thank you so much for your time man. I'll tell my kids about this story one day," he wrote.The moment struck a chord across the NFL community. Fans praised JJ Watt for making the effort instead of simply liking the post or sending a short reply. Many called it another example of why he remains one of football's most respected personalities years after retirement.One fan said, “This is such an incredible story and JJ Watt is a GOAT on and off the field.”Another fan added, “This is awesome.”The third fan added, “I love that JJ Watt likes soccer and is just a chill a** dude.”One more said, “JJ is such a f****g legend.”Another folk added, “JJ is a great football player and human being! He is definitely a GOAT! Always doing the right thing for others! You are so lucky, I’ve always wanted to meet him as I am a Houstonian and big Texans fan. He is a Special Man! Glad you got your picture!❤️👏🥰”That reputation did not come overnight. Watt spent 12 NFL seasons, mostly with the Houston Texans, and finished with 114.5 career sacks, 27 forced fumbles, five Pro Bowl selections and three Defensive Player of the Year awards. In 2017, he also received the Walter Payton NFL Man of the Year Award after raising more than $37 million for Hurricane Harvey relief.During his Walter Payton NFL Man of the Year acceptance speech at the NFL Honors ceremony in February 2018, Watt said, "When I go to my grave I want to be remembered as a guy who helped people out."His actions on July 18 showed those words still guide him. For one supporter, a respectful decision led to an unforgettable meeting. For everyone else, it became another reminder of why JJ Watt continues to earn admiration long after his playing days ended.
Article Summary: Internet can't get enough of what jj watt did for a fan who respected his privacy. The exchange quickly spread across social media, with fans praising the former nfl star for taking time out of his day. For one supporter, a respectful decision led to an unforgettable meeting.

Article Title: NBA trade rumor: Lakers get disappointing update on 6-foot-8 restricted free agent amid Clippers chase
Article Text:
Lakers and Cl​ippers. Image via: Lakers and Clippers​

Category Details Player Peyton Watson Current Team Denver Nuggets (restricted free agent) Rumored Teams Los Angeles Lakers, Los Angeles Clippers, Atlanta Hawks, Milwaukee Bucks Contract Status Restricted free agent 2025-26 Production 14.6 PPG, 4.9 RPG, 1.1 BPG Trade Likelihood (Lakers) Low Latest Insider Update Jake Fischer reports the Lakers lack the draft capital needed to meet Denver's asking price for Watson.

Which teams are interested in Peyton Watson?

Peyton Watson. Image via: Isaiah J. Downing-Imagn Images

What are insiders saying about the trade rumor?

Contract details and salary cap implications

How the trade could impact both teams

Peyton Watson turned into one of the league's most sought-after restricted free agents this summer, and the Lakers are reportedly on the outside looking in. Los Angeles has liked Watson's game since the trade deadline, but insider Jake Fischer says the front office simply doesn't have the assets to make a competitive offer to Denver.That gap widened further after the Lakers committed a stack of draft picks to land Walker Kessler from Utah in a sign-and-trade. With that capital spent, Los Angeles now finds itself watching from the sidelines as teams like the Clippers, Hawks and Bucks jockey for position on one of the top wings available.Denver's incumbent, the Hawks, the Bucks and the Clippers make up the real market for Watson, with the Lakers largely priced out. The Hawks were viewed as a strong fit given their young core, but their three-team deal with Dallas and Oklahoma City that brought in Dort and Nembhard has squeezed their roster and cap flexibility. Atlanta's frontcourt, anchored by Dyson Daniels, Jalen Johnson and Onyeka Okongwu, also leaves little room for Watson to slot in. That shift benefits the Clippers directly, trimming a serious competitor from the field. Fischer laid out the Lakers' predicament bluntly, explaining, "Well, like I just said, the Lakers don't have the draft capital to play ball with Denver's asking price. The Lakers are limited to one 2032 first-round pick swap. They don't have any first-round picks they can trade, so that's just a non-starter for Denver.”"But the Lakers have had an interest in Peyton Watson dating back to the trade deadline. So it's not that they're not interested in him. I just think the Lakers know that there is no real plausible path for them to satisfy Denver's wishes in a sign-and-trade."He noted Los Angeles is limited to a single 2032 first-round pick swap, with no outright first-rounders left to offer. Fischer added that the interest is real, but there's no plausible route to satisfying Denver's demands in a sign-and-trade scenario.Watson enters restricted free agency after a breakout Year 4, putting Denver in position to match any outside offer. The Lakers' pick capital, already stretched thin, was further depleted by the Kessler acquisition, which cost two first-round picks and two pick swaps. That leaves Los Angeles with essentially no currency for a Watson sign-and-trade.For the Lakers, the more realistic path now runs through a Jonathan Kuminga sign-and-trade with Atlanta, with Matisse Thybulle as a fallback option.For the Clippers, the Hawks' reduced involvement removes a major obstacle, though Denver's matching rights and Milwaukee's fit with Watson still loom as challenges heading into the next stretch of free agency.
Article Summary: Peyton watson is one of the most sought-after restricted free agents this summer. The lakers lack the draft capital needed to meet denver's asking price. The hawks, the bucks and the clippers make up the real market for watson.
